    Khi EMA 9 cắt xuống EMA 21 → tín hiệu bán.

Title: Trump Announces Ambition for Cryptocurrency Dominance

Description: Trump has announced the ambition to achieve dominance in the cryptocurrency sector, signaling potential strategic focus on digital assets and blockchain technology. The statement reflects recognition of cryptocurrency's growing importance in the global financial landscape.

Content: Trump has announced the ambition to achieve dominance in the cryptocurrency sector, signaling potential strategic focus on digital assets and blockchain technology. The statement reflects recognition of cryptocurrency's growing importance in the global financial landscape.
*this image is generated using AI for illustrative purposes only.
Trump has publicly announced ambitious plans for cryptocurrency leadership, stating the goal to achieve dominance in the digital asset sector.
In a recent statement, Trump declared the intention to establish dominance in the cryptocurrency space. The announcement reflects a strategic focus on digital assets and blockchain technology development.
The statement comes at a time when cryptocurrency adoption continues to expand globally. Digital assets have become increasingly important in the financial landscape, with various nations competing for leadership in blockchain innovation and cryptocurrency regulation.
Trump's announcement suggests a potential policy direction that could impact the cryptocurrency industry. The emphasis on dominance indicates recognition of the growing importance of digital assets in the global financial system.
Trump has declared that the United States will continue with its current policy approach toward Iran. The statement emphasizes maintaining the existing diplomatic and strategic framework without deviation from established protocols.
*this image is generated using AI for illustrative purposes only.
Trump has announced that the United States will maintain its current policy trajectory regarding Iran, emphasizing continuity in the nation's diplomatic approach.
According to Trump's statement, the United States will keep moving ahead with its established policy framework concerning Iran. This announcement signals the administration's intention to maintain consistency in its strategic approach toward the Middle Eastern nation.
The reaffirmation of policy continuity suggests that existing diplomatic, economic, and strategic measures will remain in place. Trump's statement indicates no immediate changes to the current bilateral relationship framework between the two countries.

Title: Bitcoin Surges to $74,000 After President Trump Throws Support Behind Key Crypto Bill

Description: The world's largest cryptocurrency has gained about 8% over the last 24 hours, hitting its highest level in a month.

Content: VCG /Getty Images
 This crypto winter could be cut short.

Bitcoin (BTCUSD) rallied over the last 24 hours, pushing prices to as high as $74,000 for the first time since early February—signs of an early thaw that appear to be giving investors hope that a recovery is underway. Altcoins, including ether (ETHUSD) and sol (SOLUSD), also rose, helping drive the overall crypto market back to $2.5 trillion in market capitalization, according to data compiled by Messari.

The surge in crypto prices coincided with President Donald Trump throwing his support behind a key crypto bill called the Clarity Act—the passing of which crypto experts have said was the sole, near-term catalyst that could pull the coin market out of its months-long rut. Meanwhile, a major investor, Cathie Wood's ARK Invest, recently picked up more shares of Coinbase (COIN) and Robinhood (HOOD), crypto-linked stocks that are also gaining on Wednesday.

"The Banks should not be trying to undercut The Genius Act, or hold the Clarity Act hostage. They need to make a good deal with the Crypto Industry because that's what's in the best interest of the American People," Trump said.

The disagreement between banks and the crypto industry is, in a nutshell, over yield. Crypto companies pay rewards on stablecoins, which aim to stay pegged to fiat like the U.S. dollar—activity that banks argue would compete with deposit accounts.
 The Clarity Act, which would establish a general framework to evaluate whether cryptocurrencies were securities or commodities, stalled in the Senate in January amid a standoff between the industry and big banks over stablecoin rewards. Trump's missive, posted to Truth Social Tuesday evening, would appear to have renewed optimism that the bill would pass.

If it does, and stablecoin rewards are allowed, crypto platforms including Coinbase, and ventures like the Trump family-backed World Liberty Financial, which has its own dollar-pegged stablecoin USD1, stand to benefit.

That crypto prices held relatively steady in spite of geopolitical tensions driving volatility in risk-assets earlier this week bodes well for the coin market.

However, elevated macroeconomic risks—widening credit spreads, and stress in private credit markets—remain headwinds, according to Sean Farrell, Fundstrat's head of digital asset strategy.

"This is likely a rally to rent rather than own," he said, in a note yesterday.

Ark Funds. "Fund documents."
Truth Social. "@realDonaldTrump, 5:02 p.m., March 3, 2026."
Fundstrat. "Digital Assets: Comments: VIX Signal and Crypto Relative Strength Support Near-Term Bullish Bias."

Source: Investopedia

Title: RCMP logo used in secondary crypto scam targeting Nanaimo victim

Description: The Nanaimo RCMP is warning the public about a cryptocurrency-related fraud that began with an online job offer and later escalated into a secondary “recovery” scam involving the misuse of the RCMP logo.

Content: Be the first to know with a daily rundown and news alerts sent straight to your inbox
CHEK

Independent and employee owned –  local news stories, weather, sports, events and more

The Nanaimo RCMP is warning the public about a cryptocurrency-related fraud that began with an online job offer and later escalated into a secondary “recovery” scam involving the misuse of the RCMP logo.
Police say the incident was reported in mid-February.
According to investigators, a Nanaimo resident received an unsolicited text message in late 2025 promoting an at-home job opportunity involving buying and selling stock using cryptocurrency. The individual was persuaded to deposit $5,000 through a cryptocurrency ATM to participate. After the payment was made, communication with the so-called employer abruptly ended and the funds were lost.
Earlier this year, the same individual encountered what appeared to be an RCMP-branded public service announcement online encouraging victims to report similar frauds. After clicking the link and completing a form, the victim received a phone call from a man identifying himself as “Sam Peterson,” who claimed to be a lawyer with a Toronto-based firm called Helprpc.
The caller alleged he had located two cryptocurrency wallets in the victim’s name containing approximately $60,000 in “profits” and offered to help recover the funds.
The victim became suspicious and verified that the caller was not registered with the Law Society of British Columbia or Ontario. Police also determined the website provided by the caller was non-functional.
“The RCMP does not contact individuals about discovered cryptocurrency accounts, partner with private firms to recover lost funds, or request any form of payment to investigate fraud. Any communication suggesting otherwise is fraudulent,” said Reserve Const. Gary O’Brien of the Nanaimo RCMP in a statement.
Police are reminding residents to be cautious of unsolicited job offers received through text message, social media or online advertisements, particularly those involving cryptocurrency or requests for deposits through crypto ATMs.
Authorities also advise residents to independently verify websites and credentials of anyone claiming to be a lawyer, investigator or financial professional through official regulatory bodies.
Anyone who believes they have been targeted by fraud is encouraged to report the incident to the Canadian Anti-Fraud Centre at 1-888-495-8501 or through its official website. Victims are also advised to notify their financial institution and obtain a credit report through Equifax or TransUnion to check for unauthorized accounts.

Title: US, European stocks rise as oil steps back and crypto rallies

Description: After some steep losses in Asian markets, U.S. and European shares were rising on Wednesday as oil prices took a step back after a dramatic two-day rally and a jump in cryptocurrency encouraged...

Content: Log In

Published on 03/04/2026
                    at 12:46 pm EST        - Modified on 03/04/2026
                    at 12:47 pm EST
By Sinéad Carew and Alun John

NEW YORK/LONDON, March 4 (Reuters) - After some steep losses in Asian markets, U.S. and European shares were rising on Wednesday as oil prices took a step back after a dramatic two-day rally and a jump in cryptocurrency encouraged investors even while they cautiously monitored the Middle East war.

The dollar fell and U.S. Treasury yields were rising for a third straight day, even as the U.S. and Israel pressed on with round-the-clock assaults on Iran. Also, a U.S. submarine sank an Iranian warship off the southern coast of Sri Lanka, and Turkey said that NATO air defences destroyed a ballistic missile fired from Iran.

Traders said that during the European session, stocks were supported by a New York Times report that operatives from Iran's Ministry of Intelligence reached out indirectly to the CIA the day after U.S. and Israeli attacks on Iran began, with an offer to discuss terms for ending the conflict. However, Iran's ambassador to the United Nations in Geneva on Tuesday had ruled out for now any negotiations with the United States.

"The market is cautiously optimistic around the Iran, US, Israel conflict, in the sense of, hopefully, it will be a short endeavor," said Ben Sullivan, chief investment officer at AE Wealth Management.

On Wall Street traders were encouraged by a dip in oil prices and a strong rally in bitcoin along with gains in the semiconductor sector and recently battered software stocks, according to Michael James, equity sales trader, Rosenblatt Securities.

"You combine all of those and it equates to a market that's feeling further emboldened," said James, adding that bitcoin's gains encouraged long-term and short-term traders to take on risk.

"The fact the stock market has rallied impressively off two gap down lows Monday and Tuesday morning, and recovered meaningfully, gave some added conviction to the bulls," he said.

In cryptocurrencies, bitcoin gained 7.77% to $73,338.07. Ethereum rose 9.15% to $2,149.09.

On Wall Street at 11:54 a.m. ET (1654 GMT) the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 329.68 points, or 0.68%, to 48,832.84, the S&P 500 rose 60.03 points, or 0.88%, to 6,876.69 and the Nasdaq Composite rose 330.30 points, or 1.47%, to 22,846.99.

MSCI's gauge of stocks across the globe rose 2.76 points, or 0.27%, to 1,032.54 while the pan-European STOXX 600 index rose 1.37%.

However, in Asia equity investors were a lot more bearish.

In South Korea, the KOSPI benchmark closed down 12%, the index's largest drop on record. South Korea is heavily reliant on Middle Eastern oil. [.KS]  While the KOSPI was still showing a more than 20% year-to-date gain, over two days the tech-heavy index has lost more than 18% of its value while the currency slumped to a 17-year low.

Japan's Nikkei fell 3.6% and Taiwan stocks dropped 4.3%.

In currencies, the U.S. dollar slipped, pulling back from the multi-month highs it touched in the previous session, as investors unwound safe-haven positions on rising hopes that the Middle East conflict may prove shorter-lived than initially feared.

The dollar index, which measures the greenback against a basket of currencies including the yen and the euro, fell 0.29% to 98.79, with the euro up 0.21% at $1.1638.

Against the Japanese yen, the dollar weakened 0.51% to 156.89 while sterling strengthened 0.09% to $1.3368.

In government bonds, U.S. Treasury yields advanced as investors gauged the likelihood of higher inflation and the path of monetary policy as the war in Iran continues to put oil prices at risk of rising further.

The yield on benchmark U.S. 10-year notes rose 1.2 basis points to 4.069%, from 4.057% late on Tuesday while the 30-year bond yield  rose 0.8 basis points to 4.7107%.

The 2-year note yield, which typically moves in step with interest rate expectations for the Federal Reserve, rose 1.6 basis points to 3.517%.

For now, oil prices paused their rally after the report about Iranian operatives seeking U.S. talks to end the Middle East conflict. Still, U.S. and Israeli strikes against Iran escalated the conflict and paralysed shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, disrupting oil shipping for a fifth day.

U.S. crude fell 0.63% to $74.09 a barrel and Brent fell to $81.06 per barrel, down 0.38% on the day.

Trading in precious metals was boosted by the pause in the dollar's rally and appeared to suggest that investors were still counting on safe-have assets because of the uncertainty brought by the war.

Spot gold rose 1.21% to $5,148.17 an ounce. U.S. gold futures rose 0.7% to $5,143.00 an ounce. Spot silver rose 2.08% to $83.73 an ounce.

(Reporting by Tom Westbrook and Rae Wee in Singapore, Alun John in London, Sinéad Carew in new York; Editing by Jacqueline Wong, Hugh Lawson, William Maclean)

Title: Mumbai Cyber Fraud: 70-Year-Old Man Duped Of ₹1.65 Crore In Cryptocurrency Investment Scam; Case Registered

Description: A 70-year-old man was duped of ₹1.65 crore in a cryptocurrency investment fraud after a scammer befriended him on Facebook and persuaded him to use a fake trading app. The victim transferred money in multiple transactions after seeing fake profits on the platform. An FIR has been registered under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita and IT Act.

Content: Mumbai, March 4: A 70-year-old man fell prey to scammers and lost Rs 1.65 crore in a cryptocurrency investment fraud. The scammer befriended the complainant on Facebook and gradually convinced him to invest in cryptocurrency and induced him to download a bogus trading app and transfer money into different beneficiary bank accounts.
Victim befriended on Facebook
According to the police, the complainant is a resident of Jalgaon. On January 29, the complainant received a Facebook friend request from an unknown woman. The complainant accepted the said request, after which the duo started regularly chatting with each other.
During one of their conversations, the scammer informed the complainant about the benefits of cryptocurrency trading and a trading app through which trading and investments could be done.
Scammer induces victim to download trading app
The scammer then induced the complainant to download a trading app on his phone and, after initially receiving profits on his investments, the complainant trusted the trading platform and decided to invest further.
From February 9 till February 17, the scammer induced the complainant to transfer Rs 1.65 crore into different beneficiary bank accounts in ten online transactions.
Fake profits displayed on trading platform
Later, when the complainant checked his trading app, he realised that his earnings had reached Rs 3.91 crore.
The complainant then told the scammer, who he had connected with over Facebook, to help him withdraw his earnings from the trading app, after which the scammer told him to pay Rs 65.31 lakh more as a verification fee to get his money.
Scammer refuses to return money
Since the complainant did not have more money, he kept requesting the scammer to return his money, but the scammer kept giving evasive replies and refused to return his money until he paid Rs 65.31 lakh, police said.
Also Watch: 
FIR registered in cyber fraud case
Having realised that he had been duped, the complainant raised a complaint on the cyber crime portal, following which an FIR has been registered under Section 318 (cheating) of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita and Section 66D (cheating by personation by using computer resource) of the Information Technology Act in the matter.

Title: Political Pressure on Binance: A Threat to Decentralization and Financial Freedom

Description: Introduction: A Political Probe into Crypto’s Largest Exchange In a stark escalation of political pressure on the cryptocurrency industry, eleven Senate Democrats have demanded the Trump administration’s Justice Department and Treasury investigate Binance, the world’s largest digital asset exchange. The lawmakers, led by Senator Mark Warner and including prominent crypto-skeptic Elizabeth Warren, cite allegations that […]

Content: In a stark escalation of political pressure on the cryptocurrency industry, eleven Senate Democrats have demanded the Trump administration's Justice Department and Treasury investigate Binance, the world's largest digital asset exchange.

The lawmakers, led by Senator Mark Warner and including prominent crypto-skeptic Elizabeth Warren, cite allegations that the platform facilitated billions in transactions linked to Iranian entities and question its compliance with a 2023 federal settlement. [1][2][3]

This move, framed as a national security imperative, represents far more than a routine regulatory inquiry; it is a targeted political maneuver that threatens the foundational principles of financial privacy and decentralized economic freedom.

The probe is not merely about enforcement; it is a strategic assault on a pivotal gateway to peer-to-peer finance. By weaponizing sanctions compliance as a pretext, these senators aim to paint the entire crypto ecosystem as a threat, thereby justifying greater centralized control.

As one analysis notes, "Operation Choke Point 2.0 is underway, and crypto is in its crosshairs." [4] This pattern of using state power to choke off access to decentralized tools is a direct challenge to the human right to transact freely without government surveillance or political interference.

The central thrust of the senators' letter revolves around media reports alleging that roughly $1.7 billion in digital assets flowed through Binance to Iranian entities, including groups like the Houthis and the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps. [1][5][6] They contend that this activity, and the alleged dismissal of compliance staff who identified it, calls into question whether Binance has upheld its sweeping 2023 plea agreement, which included over $4 billion in penalties and commitments to enhanced oversight. [1][6] The lawmakers warn that "poor illicit finance controls at Binance remain a significant threat to national security." [7]

This framing is deliberate and dangerous. By defining cryptocurrency primarily as a vector for illicit finance and a national security threat, the state seeks to legitimize aggressive intervention. It shifts the narrative away from crypto's core value proposition: enabling individual sovereignty over money.

The focus on centralized exchanges like Binance is a tactical choice, as they represent a chokepoint where government pressure can be most effectively applied. The goal is to herd users away from decentralized, peer-to-peer systems and into heavily monitored, bank-like channels where every transaction can be tracked, controlled and potentially blocked for political reasons.

Crucially, the senators' letter injects partisan politics directly into the financial regulatory sphere by highlighting Binance's connections to President Donald Trump. They point to the exchange's promotion of USD1, a stablecoin issued by a Trump family-backed project, and reference President Trump's pardon last fall of Binance founder Changpeng Zhao. [1][5] These connections, the lawmakers argue, necessitate a "thorough, impartial" probe. [1] This is a transparent attempt to cast the investigation as a necessary check on corruption, but in reality, it politicizes the very act of financial innovation and association.

This tactic mirrors a long-standing pattern where political opposition weaponizes regulatory and legal apparatuses against adversaries. As noted in broader analyses of power, “vested interests have ensured that earmarks litter important legislation” and that “the lust for a quick profit and lax regulation” often drives state action. [8] By framing a business relationship with a political figure as inherently suspect, the probe seeks to chill innovation and punish platforms that operate outside the approved corridors of Washington power. It is an effort to extend political warfare into the realm of financial tools, turning a decentralized exchange into a pawn in a partisan battle.

The attack on Binance is not an isolated incident but a key front in a larger war against decentralized finance. This pattern, described as "Operation Choke Point 2.0," involves a "highly coordinated" crackdown across financial regulators designed to render crypto businesses "completely unbanked." [4] The ultimate aim is to enforce a regime of total financial surveillance and centralize monetary control, mirroring the push for Central Bank Digital Currencies (CBDCs) that would give governments absolute authority over spending.

This agenda represents the antithesis of financial freedom. Decentralized cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin were created precisely to escape the corruption and control inherent in centralized fiat systems. As discussed by financial experts, when people store value on centralized exchanges vulnerable to government interference, it "undermines its potential for financial freedom." [9] The state's strategy is clear: attack the largest, most visible custodial gateways to create fear, uncertainty and doubt, thereby discouraging public adoption of technologies that empower individuals. It is a modern-day manifestation of what historian Janine Wedel observed in other contexts, where state and commercial interests become so intertwined that "the respective agendas of the state and the clan sometimes seemed identical." [10]

At its core, this political pressure campaign threatens fundamental human rights. The capability for private, peer-to-peer transaction is a cornerstone of individual liberty, akin to free speech. Cryptocurrency offers an escape from a financial system rigged by "international bankers" and prone to endless fiat currency debasement. [11] As gold and silver advocate Andy Schectman has noted, precious metals – and by extension, honest digital money – represent “intrinsic value” in an increasingly digital and manipulated world. [12] Politically-motivated probes based on tenuous connections set a dangerous precedent for using law enforcement to target technological dissent.

The corruption within the current fiat system is well-documented. From the engineered inflation that steals wealth from savers to the weaponization of banking against political enemies, centralized control of money is a tool of tyranny. [4]

The push to regulate and surveil crypto seeks to extend this corrupt model into the digital realm. It aims to replace the promise of decentralized, "honest money" with a new, digitally-facilitated form of central bank control that would make financial privacy impossible and dissent financially untenable.

The Senate Democrats' probe into Binance is a political maneuver dressed in the garb of law enforcement. Its true purpose is not to enhance security but to assert control over a financial frontier that challenges state monopoly on money.

As the global debt machine nears its inevitable collapse and trust in fiat currencies erodes, decentralized alternatives pose an existential threat to those in power. [13] This investigation is a tactical move in a larger strategy to chill innovation and herd users back into surveilled, controlled financial channels.

Defending financial sovereignty requires vigilance and action. Individuals must educate themselves on the importance of self-custody, supporting technologies and platforms that prioritize privacy and resist centralized coercion.

Seeking out uncensored information sources like BrightNews.ai for news trends and BrightAnswers.ai for honest AI analysis is crucial in an era of pervasive propaganda. The future of economic freedom depends on recognizing these politically-motivated encroachments for what they are and choosing to exit a corrupt system to build decentralized, human-centric alternatives.

Title: From Mining to Staking: Choosing the Right Blockchain for Business Transactions

Description: As cryptocurrency becomes integrated into global commerce, businesses are asking which blockchain to use. The choice between networks based on mining and those built on staking can significantly influence transaction costs, processing speed, scalability, and sustainability.

Content: As cryptocurrency becomes integrated into global commerce, businesses are asking which blockchain to use. The choice between networks based on mining and those built on staking can significantly influence transaction costs, processing speed, scalability, and sustainability.
Understanding how consensus mechanisms work is essential for companies that rely on crypto for payments, cross-border transfers, or treasury management. From transaction confirmation times to network security, the underlying structure of a blockchain directly affects business performance.
At the heart of blockchain networks lies a consensus mechanism. This is the system that validates transactions and secures the network. The two most widely used mechanisms today are mining-based and staking-based models, commonly compared as Proof of Work (PoW) vs Proof of Stake (PoS).
In a Proof of Work system, transactions are validated by miners who race to encode transactions within a format that meets the network’s size requirements. This model underpins networks like Bitcoin and has a long track record of security. However, it can require significant energy consumption and may result in slower confirmation times during periods of network congestion.
In contrast, Proof of Stake networks validate transactions through participants who lock up (stake) their cryptocurrency as collateral. Validators are selected to confirm blocks based on the size of their stake and other factors. This model is generally more energy-efficient and often allows for higher transaction throughput.
For businesses, the choice between PoW and PoS networks influences both operational efficiency and long-term sustainability.
Security remains a primary concern when selecting a blockchain for transactions. PoW networks are often considered highly secure due to the enormous computational power required to compromise them. The cost of launching a majority attack on established PoW chains can be prohibitively high.
PoS networks, meanwhile, rely on economic incentives rather than computational difficulty. Validators risk losing their staked assets if they act maliciously. While some businesses may perceive PoS as newer or less battle-tested, many modern PoS networks have matured significantly and are widely adopted for commercial applications.
Companies handling high-value transfers or treasury reserves should evaluate:
Security must align with the organization’s transaction volume and risk tolerance.
Beyond security, transaction speed and cost are critical operational factors. PoW networks can experience congestion during peak demand, leading to higher fees and slower confirmation times. This can impact customer experience in retail or subscription-based environments.
PoS networks often offer:
For businesses executing frequent transfers, payroll distributions, or supplier payments, these performance differences can directly affect operational costs.
When companies need to execute digital asset transfers efficiently, solutions like https://cryptoprocessing.com/send-crypto can streamline outbound crypto transactions across supported networks. The ability to send funds quickly and reliably becomes even more important when operating internationally.
Environmental considerations have become increasingly relevant in corporate decision-making. PoW networks, due to their energy-intensive mining processes, have faced scrutiny regarding their environmental impact.
PoS networks are often seen as more energy-efficient alternatives because they do not require large-scale computational mining. For businesses with sustainability goals or ESG commitments, blockchain selection may extend beyond technical metrics to include environmental alignment.
Choosing a network aligned with corporate responsibility objectives can strengthen brand reputation and meet stakeholder expectations.
Regulatory perspectives on blockchain technology are in flux. Some jurisdictions may scrutinize certain consensus models differently depending on their structure and governance.
Businesses should consider:
Networks with broad exchange support and established infrastructure may offer smoother compliance integration.
Different business models require different blockchain characteristics.
For example:
Rather than viewing mining and staking as competing ideologies, businesses should assess how each mechanism aligns with operational needs.
Consensus choice is only one component of a broader crypto strategy. Businesses must ensure their blockchain selection integrates smoothly with payment gateways, wallet infrastructure, and reporting systems.
A well-designed crypto payment stack includes:
Without integration capabilities, even the most efficient blockchain network may create operational friction.
Secure asset storage also requires businesses to determine what type of wallet infrastructure best fits their operational model. Companies must decide whether to retain full control over private keys or rely on a third-party provider to manage custody and security processes. Understanding the differences between a custodial vs non-custodial wallet approach helps businesses align wallet management with their internal risk policies, compliance requirements, and treasury strategy.
Blockchain ecosystems continue to evolve. Upgrades, network forks, staking reward adjustments, and regulatory shifts can affect network dynamics over time.
Businesses should evaluate:
Selecting a blockchain shouldn’t be part of the short-term plan. It should support sustainable growth over years.
From mining-based security to staking-driven efficiency, the choice between blockchain consensus models has meaningful implications for business transactions. Understanding the differences between Proof of Work and Proof of Stake enables companies to make informed decisions aligned with their goals.
Rather than asking which model is universally superior, businesses should evaluate how each network’s characteristics align with their transaction volume, risk tolerance, compliance requirements, and growth strategy.
Ultimately, selecting the right blockchain is an important step toward building resilient and scalable crypto-powered operations.

Title: President Trump slams banks for blocking crypto bill progress

Description: President Trump has strongly criticized banks for allegedly obstructing progress on the country’s cryptocurrency market structure legislation.

Content: 
        To keep going please Log in.

President Donald Trump has strongly criticized banks for allegedly obstructing progress on the country’s cryptocurrency market structure legislation.
In a Truth Social post in March, Trump accused banking groups of attempting to undermine the GENIUS Act, a stablecoin regulation law passed by Congress in July.
According to Trump, the banking sector’s push to modify the bill threatens a key pillar of his broader crypto policy agenda.
Trump argued that the U.S. should move quickly to finalize market structure policies for the digital asset industry.
He warned that delays could push cryptocurrency businesses to relocate operations to China or other jurisdictions that provide clearer regulatory frameworks. Trump also emphasized that banks, despite reporting record profits, should not interfere with policies designed to support the crypto sector and maintain U.S. leadership in financial innovation.
At the center of the dispute is the issue of stablecoin yield payments. The GENIUS Act allows stablecoin issuers to operate under a regulated framework but prevents them from directly paying interest or yield to token holders. 
However, third-party platforms such as cryptocurrency exchanges can still provide yield opportunities to customers holding stablecoins.
Banking groups argue that this arrangement creates a loophole that could draw significant deposits away from traditional bank accounts into digital assets.
They have therefore been lobbying lawmakers to expand the ban on yield payments to cover all types of stablecoin-related returns.
Crypto industry representatives, however, oppose such restrictions, arguing that yield opportunities are critical to the competitiveness and growth of the digital asset ecosystem.
Notably, the dispute has already delayed legislative progress. The Senate Banking Committee postponed consideration of the market structure bill after major crypto lobbyists, including Coinbase, withdrew support earlier this year over disagreements about yield restrictions.
Negotiations between banking and crypto representatives have continued, including White House meetings, but no final compromise has been reached.
Meanwhile, Representative French Hill has urged the Senate to consider the House-passed CLARITY Act if it cannot agree on its own version. 
He emphasized that the House bill drew bipartisan support and reinforced that stablecoins should primarily serve as blockchain-based payment tools, not investment products.
With midterm elections approaching, pressure is mounting on lawmakers to deliver a framework that balances financial stability with crypto innovation.

In a recent statement, Trump declared the intention to establish dominance in the cryptocurrency space. The announcement reflects a strategic focus on digital assets and blockchain technology development.
The statement comes at a time when cryptocurrency adoption continues to expand globally. Digital assets have become increasingly important in the financial landscape, with various nations competing for leadership in blockchain innovation and cryptocurrency regulation.
Trump's announcement suggests a potential policy direction that could impact the cryptocurrency industry. The emphasis on dominance indicates recognition of the growing importance of digital assets in the global financial system.

According to Trump's statement, the United States will keep moving ahead with its established policy framework concerning Iran. This announcement signals the administration's intention to maintain consistency in its strategic approach toward the Middle Eastern nation.
The reaffirmation of policy continuity suggests that existing diplomatic, economic, and strategic measures will remain in place. Trump's statement indicates no immediate changes to the current bilateral relationship framework between the two countries.

Published: 2026-03-04T20:47:57Z

The Nanaimo RCMP is warning the public about a cryptocurrency-related fraud that began with an online job offer and later escalated into a secondary “recovery” scam involving the misuse of the RCMP logo.
Police say the incident was reported in mid-February.
According to investigators, a Nanaimo resident received an unsolicited text message in late 2025 promoting an at-home job opportunity involving buying and selling stock using cryptocurrency. The individual was persuaded to deposit $5,000 through a cryptocurrency ATM to participate. After the payment was made, communication with the so-called employer abruptly ended and the funds were lost.
Earlier this year, the same individual encountered what appeared to be an RCMP-branded public service announcement online encouraging victims to report similar frauds. After clicking the link and completing a form, the victim received a phone call from a man identifying himself as “Sam Peterson,” who claimed to be a lawyer with a Toronto-based firm called Helprpc.
The caller alleged he had located two cryptocurrency wallets in the victim’s name containing approximately $60,000 in “profits” and offered to help recover the funds.
The victim became suspicious and verified that the caller was not registered with the Law Society of British Columbia or Ontario. Police also determined the website provided by the caller was non-functional.
“The RCMP does not contact individuals about discovered cryptocurrency accounts, partner with private firms to recover lost funds, or request any form of payment to investigate fraud. Any communication suggesting otherwise is fraudulent,” said Reserve Const. Gary O’Brien of the Nanaimo RCMP in a statement.
Police are reminding residents to be cautious of unsolicited job offers received through text message, social media or online advertisements, particularly those involving cryptocurrency or requests for deposits through crypto ATMs.
Authorities also advise residents to independently verify websites and credentials of anyone claiming to be a lawyer, investigator or financial professional through official regulatory bodies.
Anyone who believes they have been targeted by fraud is encouraged to report the incident to the Canadian Anti-Fraud Centre at 1-888-495-8501 or through its official website. Victims are also advised to notify their financial institution and obtain a credit report through Equifax or TransUnion to check for unauthorized accounts.

Published: 2026-03-04T19:45:00Z

In government bonds, U.S. Treasury yields advanced as investors gauged the likelihood of higher inflation and the path of monetary policy as the war in Iran continues to put oil prices at risk of rising further.

The yield on benchmark U.S. 10-year notes rose 1.2 basis points to 4.069%, from 4.057% late on Tuesday while the 30-year bond yield  rose 0.8 basis points to 4.7107%.

The 2-year note yield, which typically moves in step with interest rate expectations for the Federal Reserve, rose 1.6 basis points to 3.517%.

For now, oil prices paused their rally after the report about Iranian operatives seeking U.S. talks to end the Middle East conflict. Still, U.S. and Israeli strikes against Iran escalated the conflict and paralysed shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, disrupting oil shipping for a fifth day.

U.S. crude fell 0.63% to $74.09 a barrel and Brent fell to $81.06 per barrel, down 0.38% on the day.

Trading in precious metals was boosted by the pause in the dollar's rally and appeared to suggest that investors were still counting on safe-have assets because of the uncertainty brought by the war.

Spot gold rose 1.21% to $5,148.17 an ounce. U.S. gold futures rose 0.7% to $5,143.00 an ounce. Spot silver rose 2.08% to $83.73 an ounce.

(Reporting by Tom Westbrook and Rae Wee in Singapore, Alun John in London, Sinéad Carew in new York; Editing by Jacqueline Wong, Hugh Lawson, William Maclean)

This tactic mirrors a long-standing pattern where political opposition weaponizes regulatory and legal apparatuses against adversaries. As noted in broader analyses of power, “vested interests have ensured that earmarks litter important legislation” and that “the lust for a quick profit and lax regulation” often drives state action. [8] By framing a business relationship with a political figure as inherently suspect, the probe seeks to chill innovation and punish platforms that operate outside the approved corridors of Washington power. It is an effort to extend political warfare into the realm of financial tools, turning a decentralized exchange into a pawn in a partisan battle.

The attack on Binance is not an isolated incident but a key front in a larger war against decentralized finance. This pattern, described as "Operation Choke Point 2.0," involves a "highly coordinated" crackdown across financial regulators designed to render crypto businesses "completely unbanked." [4] The ultimate aim is to enforce a regime of total financial surveillance and centralize monetary control, mirroring the push for Central Bank Digital Currencies (CBDCs) that would give governments absolute authority over spending.

This agenda represents the antithesis of financial freedom. Decentralized cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin were created precisely to escape the corruption and control inherent in centralized fiat systems. As discussed by financial experts, when people store value on centralized exchanges vulnerable to government interference, it "undermines its potential for financial freedom." [9] The state's strategy is clear: attack the largest, most visible custodial gateways to create fear, uncertainty and doubt, thereby discouraging public adoption of technologies that empower individuals. It is a modern-day manifestation of what historian Janine Wedel observed in other contexts, where state and commercial interests become so intertwined that "the respective agendas of the state and the clan sometimes seemed identical." [10]

At its core, this political pressure campaign threatens fundamental human rights. The capability for private, peer-to-peer transaction is a cornerstone of individual liberty, akin to free speech. Cryptocurrency offers an escape from a financial system rigged by "international bankers" and prone to endless fiat currency debasement. [11] As gold and silver advocate Andy Schectman has noted, precious metals – and by extension, honest digital money – represent “intrinsic value” in an increasingly digital and manipulated world. [12] Politically-motivated probes based on tenuous connections set a dangerous precedent for using law enforcement to target technological dissent.

The corruption within the current fiat system is well-documented. From the engineered inflation that steals wealth from savers to the weaponization of banking against political enemies, centralized control of money is a tool of tyranny. [4]

The push to regulate and surveil crypto seeks to extend this corrupt model into the digital realm. It aims to replace the promise of decentralized, "honest money" with a new, digitally-facilitated form of central bank control that would make financial privacy impossible and dissent financially untenable.

The Senate Democrats' probe into Binance is a political maneuver dressed in the garb of law enforcement. Its true purpose is not to enhance security but to assert control over a financial frontier that challenges state monopoly on money.

As the global debt machine nears its inevitable collapse and trust in fiat currencies erodes, decentralized alternatives pose an existential threat to those in power. [13] This investigation is a tactical move in a larger strategy to chill innovation and herd users back into surveilled, controlled financial channels.

Defending financial sovereignty requires vigilance and action. Individuals must educate themselves on the importance of self-custody, supporting technologies and platforms that prioritize privacy and resist centralized coercion.

Seeking out uncensored information sources like BrightNews.ai for news trends and BrightAnswers.ai for honest AI analysis is crucial in an era of pervasive propaganda. The future of economic freedom depends on recognizing these politically-motivated encroachments for what they are and choosing to exit a corrupt system to build decentralized, human-centric alternatives.
